title: Neighborhood Zine Studio
summary: A hands-on evening where neighbors draft tiny zines about favorite local spots and swap ideas for future community events.
fullDescription: Participants spend ninety minutes making one-page zines, sharing short neighborhood recommendations, and leaving with a printed mini-guide. The organizer will bring paper, pens, and collage supplies. The workshop is designed for beginners and is meant to feed a small community bulletin feature later that week.
locationLabel: Maple Corner Library Terrace Room (Maple Grove)
audienceLabel: Older teens and adults who want a low-pressure creative session
logisticsSummary: Capacity 18; materials: paper, pens, magazine clippings, glue sticks; accessibility: Need to confirm whether the side entrance elevator will be available that evening.
facilitatorBio: Maya coordinates the neighborhood bulletin table and runs monthly storytelling circles at the library.
publicationGoal: Create a website listing, a newsletter teaser, and a short note for the volunteer coordinators.
materialSummary: paper, pens, magazine clippings, glue sticks
